decisions.title: Backend language / runtime

options.label: TypeScript / Node (Hono or Fastify)
options.description: One language with dashboard + backend, easy contract type-sharing, deploy on Vercel Functions.

options.label: Go
options.description: Single fast binary, great for stateless validation hot path; separate deploy story from dashboard.

decisions.resolution: TypeScript/Node (Hono or Fastify) + pg on Vercel Functions. Decided by Joseph 2026-06-16; unblocks the auth chain for backend to build. iOS codes to api-contract.md regardless.
decisions.detail: All auth endpoints and everything downstream need a runtime before they can be built. Now the critical path: blocks all 4 M0 auth tasks and transitively ios-auth-ui (Cursor's next task).
decisions.recommendation: TypeScript/Node (Hono or Fastify) + pg on Vercel Functions — one language across dashboard + backend, easy contract type-sharing, trivial deploy. Alternative: Go for a single fast binary off serverless.

decisions.title: Which media object storage provider?
decisions.detail: POST /memories is live and returns a signed PUT URL, but the storage backend is still a stub (placeholder URLs). Needs a real provider before uploads work on a real device. Options: Vercel Blob (zero config, same Vercel account), Cloudflare R2 (no egress fees), or AWS S3 (most flexible). Recommendation: Vercel Blob for Phase 1 — already on Vercel, no extra accounts, can swap to R2 if egress costs bite at scale. Once decided, add STORAGE_BACKEND=vercel-blob|r2|s3 + credentials to backend/.env.local and backend will implement the signed URL generation same session.

options.label: Vercel Blob
options.description: Zero-config on Vercel, same billing, client-upload token flow built-in. Swap to R2 later if needed.

options.label: Cloudflare R2
options.description: No egress fees at scale, S3-compatible. Requires a separate Cloudflare account.

options.label: AWS S3
options.description: Most flexible, PhotoDNA native path. Has egress costs and adds another vendor.

responses.author: joseph
responses.text: vercel blob is fine but we prolly want to use aws s3 for later iterations

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Implemented Vercel Blob client-upload handshake: POST /v1/uploads (handleUpload) + onUploadCompleted flips scan_status. POST /memories returns upload:null for Blob; client uses the handshake. Media served as public unguessable URL (Phase-1 trade-off, revisit before public-tier). S3 presigned path kept for later. iOS handshake + env (BLOB_READ_WRITE_TOKEN) documented in api-contract §3.2.

decisions.title: App Attest field — null ok until M5?
decisions.detail: The API contract shows an attestation field on POST /memories and POST /memories/:id/unlock. iOS currently sends null for both. Needs backend confirmation that null/omitted attestation is accepted for M1-M4 and enforcement only lands at M5 (ios-app-attest task). This affects whether iOS needs to guard or stub the field earlier than planned.

responses.author: backend
responses.text: Confirmed — backend accepts null/omitted attestation for all endpoints in M1-M4. No enforcement, no 4xx. The app_attest_required feature flag (app-attest-feature-flag task) controls enforcement and defaults to false. M5 is the right time to harden this. iOS can send null safely until then.

decisions.title: Share API contract as generated types (OpenAPI)
decisions.detail: Both sides hand-write types against api-contract.md today, which creates drift risk. Proposal: keep a checked-in OpenAPI 3.1 doc as single source of truth. Backend generates TS types, iOS uses swift-openapi-generator. Not urgent — hand-written models match v1 today. Best time to set up is when the contract first churns significantly (likely M3 seal/condition shapes). Until then: contract.md is law, update it before code.

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Agreed. Hand-wrote ~10 Codable structs against the markdown — exactly the drift surface described. Preference: OpenAPI 3.1 doc. Not urgent; set it up when the contract first churns so we do not pay setup cost twice. Rule until then: contract.md is law.

decisions.title: Warmth signal — keep 3 bands, no continuous scalar
decisions.detail: Considered adding a continuous warmth_level 0.0-1.0 for smoother gradient animation. Would it break the DEC-15 privacy invariant?

responses.author: ios
responses.text: Do not add a scalar. A responsive 0-1 distance proxy reintroduces the DEC-15 attack: walk 3 steps, read the value, repeat from two or three positions and you have trilaterated the pin without passing a proximity check. The vector/scalar distinction does not save us — trilateration only needs distances from known points, not bearings. Keep 3 coarse bands. Smooth UX comes from client-side easing between band transitions (already in WarmthCueOverlay, ~0.6s cosmetic interpolation). The user sees a smooth bloom; the device only ever knew coarse/approaching/in_bubble. Also want the bands debounced server-side so rapid in/out jitter near a boundary cannot be sampled as a fine signal.

decisions.title: Vercel Blob media served as public unguessable URL (not short-TTL)
decisions.detail: Vercel Blob has no S3-style presigned/expiring GET. Phase-1 implementation uploads media access:public with addRandomSuffix, so the blob URL is an unguessable bearer capability stored as media_key and returned on unlock. Trade-off vs the contract intent of a short-TTL signed GET: a leaked URL stays valid indefinitely; there is no server-enforced view window. Acceptable for Phase-1 private-tier memories (URL is unguessable, memories are owner/recipient-scoped), but it weakens the privacy posture for public-tier. MUST revisit before public memories / Phase 3 — options then: migrate to S3 presigned GET, or proxy private blobs through the function with a short-lived token. Documented in api-contract §3.2 and DEC-23 revisit trigger.

responses.author: joseph
responses.text: I think for pahse 3 we migrate so aws s3

manualTests.title: Cold launch shows sign-in when logged out
steps: Delete the app from the simulator (or reset Keychain for Legacy)
Run Legacy from Xcode (⌘R)
Confirm the welcome / sign-in screen appears — not the Wander map

manualTests.title: Email OTP sign-in works in DEBUG (stubbed API)
steps: Run a DEBUG build (LegacyAPIClient.stubbed())
Choose email sign-in, enter any email, request OTP
Enter the OTP from fixture / stub response
Complete DOB picker if shown
Confirm you land on the empty Wander map

manualTests.title: Under-13 DOB shows age-restricted screen
steps: Sign out or fresh install
Start sign-in and pick a DOB that makes the user under 13
Confirm age-restricted / rejection UI — must not reach Wander

manualTests.title: Session persists across relaunch (Keychain)
steps: Sign in successfully
Stop the app (not delete) and relaunch from Xcode
Confirm you skip sign-in and go straight to Wander — token read from Keychain

manualTests.title: Post-auth Wander shows empty map shell
steps: Sign in (DEBUG stub or live backend)
Confirm Wander tab is default / reachable
Map loads with no foreign-memory pins (M0 demo: empty or own pins only)

manualTests.title: Memory Lane grid loads own memories
steps: Sign in with an account that has memories (or stub fixtures with list data)
Open Memory Lane tab
Confirm teaser cards appear oldest-first; tap one opens detail
Pull to refresh or scroll — pagination / error banner behaves correctly

manualTests.title: Tab bar navigation between Wander and Memory Lane
steps: After sign-in, switch between main tabs (Wander, Memory Lane, etc.)
Confirm no crash, state preserved when switching back
Sign-out (if exposed in UI) returns to auth flow

manualTests.title: Location permission prompt on first Wander use
steps: Fresh install or reset location privacy for Legacy
Open Wander and trigger a scan (move or wait for movement gate)
Confirm iOS location permission alert appears
Allow — confirm scan loop continues without crash

tasks.title: Apply to CSAM scanning vendor (PhotoDNA/Thorn/Hive)
tasks.notes: PhotoDNA application submitted 2026-06-16. Approval is the long pole — everything else builds around the stub.

tasks.title: Write API contract doc — all endpoints, exact request/response shapes, error codes, headers
tasks.notes: Unblocks iOS APIClient work. Cursor codes to this contract before the real API exists.

tasks.title: GitHub Actions CI — lint, type-check, run migrations against local Postgres, privacy gate grep
tasks.notes: Done 2026-06-17: .github/workflows/ci.yml — backend typecheck + vitest + privacy gate grep (bans lat/lng/geohash in audit_log migrations). Runs on push/PR to main.

tasks.title: DB schema: users + sessions tables — id, dob, created_at, device_id, session_token, expires_at
tasks.notes: DOB stored at signup for age gate. Sessions are stateless bearer tokens — no server-side session store.

tasks.title: DB schema: memories table — id, owner_id, lat, lng, geohash, source, scan_status, discoverable_after, privacy_tier, media_type, created_at
tasks.notes: geohash precision 5 (~4.9km cells). Coordinates immutable after insert. scan_status: pending|clear|blocked.

tasks.title: DB schema: seals + conditions tables with DB constraint: condition_time_fallback NOT NULL when condition_type set
tasks.notes: Seal types: none, fixed_date, duration, age_based, recurring. Condition types: time_of_day, season, weather, co_presence, long_absence, nth_return. DB constraint enforces no user can be stranded.

tasks.title: DB schema: finds table (memory_id, user_id, found_at) + presence_pings UNLOGGED table (memory_id, user_id, last_seen_at)
tasks.notes: finds tracks nth_return counts. presence_pings is UNLOGGED (no WAL) with ~3min TTL — auto-purge job keeps it empty at steady state.

tasks.title: DB schema: audit_log table — event, actor_id, request_id, metadata (jsonb), created_at. No lat/lng ever.
tasks.notes: CI gate enforces coordinates never appear in this table. IP + timestamp + user_id sufficient for rate-limit abuse detection.

tasks.title: Auth: Apple Sign In — validate identity token, extract sub + email, create/fetch user
tasks.notes: Done: verifyAppleToken (JWKS RS256, iss+aud check) in src/lib/jwt.ts; user find-or-create in src/db/users.ts.

tasks.title: Auth: Google Sign In — validate ID token, extract sub + email, create/fetch user
tasks.notes: Done: verifyGoogleToken (JWKS RS256, iss+aud check) in src/lib/jwt.ts.

tasks.title: Auth: email OTP — generate 6-digit code, send via email, verify within 10min window
tasks.notes: Done: src/db/otp.ts (sha-256 hashed, single-use, attempt-capped, 10min TTL) + src/lib/email.ts (Resend or dev console). Migration 0007_email_otp.sql. Send rate-limit still TODO (M5 rate-limiting task).

tasks.title: Auth: age gate — collect DOB at token exchange, reject under-13, flag 13-15 for parental consent path
tasks.notes: Done: src/lib/age.ts resolveAgeTier — under-13 throws 403 age_restricted (never created); 13-15 minor; 16+ adult. 8 unit tests in test/age.test.ts. Parental-consent flow for minors deferred to Phase 2.

tasks.title: Auth: session token issuance + validation middleware — JWT, expiry, clock-skew tolerance
tasks.notes: Done: signSession/verifySession (HS256, ~30d) in src/lib/jwt.ts; requireAuth + clockSkew (±5min) middleware in src/middleware/auth.ts. Full auth routes in src/routes/auth.ts. typecheck + tests green.

tasks.title: iOS: SPM module scaffold — LocationEngine, APIClient, DropFeature, WanderFeature, MemoryLaneFeature, ImportFeature, DesignSystem as local packages
tasks.notes: Empty shells with correct public interfaces. No TCA. @Observable MVVM throughout. Min iOS 17.

tasks.title: iOS: DesignSystem module — typography, colors, spacing tokens, warmth gradient component, button styles
tasks.notes: Warmth gradient is screen-edge ambient only — no directional component ever.

tasks.title: iOS: APIClient base — typed request builder, Authorization header injection, X-Request-Timestamp, error model, 401 surfacing
tasks.notes: Session token read from Keychain. Codes to the contract doc — not the real API yet.

tasks.title: iOS: Keychain session storage — store/read/delete session token securely (kSecAttrAccessibleAfterFirstUnlock)
tasks.notes: SEC compliance. Token never stored in UserDefaults or disk.

tasks.title: iOS: Auth screens — Apple/Google sign-in buttons, email OTP entry, DOB picker, age gate rejection screen
tasks.notes: AuthFeature module: Apple (AuthenticationServices), email OTP, DOB gate, age-restricted screen. Google button UI-only until OAuth client ID + backend. DEBUG uses LegacyAPIClient.stubbed(); live Apple needs Sign in with Apple capability + auth-apple-oauth.

tasks.title: Backend: GET /memories — paginated owner list for Memory Lane (teaser shape, no coordinates, oldest-first)
tasks.notes: Proposed in collab-log Open questions. Response: { memories: [...], next_cursor }. Same teaser fields as /scan plus scan_status. Blocks ios-memory-lane live data.

tasks.title: Backend: POST /memories — validate input, enforce cooldown, create memory record (scan_status: pending), return memory_id
tasks.notes: Done 2026-06-17: src/routes/memories.ts. Validates coords + accuracy_m + media_type, geohash at precision 9, inserts record, returns memory_id + signed_put_url + expires_at. Text memories skip signed URL. Storage backend abstracted (STORAGE_BACKEND env var; stub active).

tasks.title: Backend: S3 signed PUT URL generation — generate per-asset URL, 15-min TTL, return alongside memory_id
tasks.notes: Abstraction in place (src/lib/storage.ts). Stub active. Needs Joseph to pick provider + add credentials. See collab-log.md open questions.

tasks.title: Backend: Server-side EXIF strip on storage webhook — belt-and-braces metadata removal after client upload
tasks.notes: Client already strips (privacy guarantee). Server strips again (guarantee of guarantee). SEC-MED-4.

tasks.title: Backend: CSAM pipeline stub — storage webhook flips scan_status to 'clear' immediately in dev/staging
tasks.notes: Real vendor slot is a no-op stub until approval. Pending assets invisible to non-owners. Owner sees own pending media.

tasks.title: Backend: Server-side thumbnail generation — fires post-clear, stores compressed thumbnail key alongside original
tasks.notes: Thumbnails used for teaser cards in scan response. Never generated for pending/blocked assets.

tasks.title: iOS: V1 Pin — camera capture + photo library picker, single photo selection, preview before drop
tasks.notes: PhotoLibraryPicker (PHPicker) + CameraPicker (UIImagePickerController) + preview + confirm drop wired in DropFeatureRootView.

tasks.title: iOS: Client-side EXIF strip — rewrite image via ImageIO, remove all metadata before upload
tasks.notes: Privacy guarantee. Core ImageIO rewrite host-verifiable without Xcode; picker wiring follows ios-v1-camera-picker.

tasks.title: iOS: Background URLSession upload — PUT to signed URL, resumable, progress tracking, retry on failure
tasks.notes: `BackgroundMediaUploader` (URLSession background config) + foreground `URLSessionMediaUploader`. App delegate completion handler + progress UI still TODO.

tasks.title: iOS: SwiftData draft store — persist interrupted drops (camera roll picked, not yet uploaded)
tasks.notes: `DropDraft` SwiftData model + Application Support photo files. Auto-save on upload failure; retry banner in Drop tab. No coordinates stored.

tasks.title: iOS: V1 Pin drop end-to-end — 1-tap: capture → strip EXIF → POST /memories → background upload → confirmation
tasks.notes: Picker + preview + DropCoordinator + SwiftData draft retry shipped. DEBUG stubs E2E works; live drops need STORAGE_BACKEND credentials.

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — geohash prefix query to find nearby memories within coarse bounding box
tasks.notes: Query geohash prefix (precision 5) + adjacent cells. O(memories near you), not O(all memories).

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — eligibility filter: privacy_tier check (owner, recipient, friend, public+age), scan_status=clear, discoverable_after elapsed
tasks.notes: Phase 1: owner-only (private tier). Recipient/friend tiers in Phase 2.

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — asymmetric proximity bubble: own memories base 25m + min(accuracy,75m) cushion; others' 20m + min(accuracy,25m), reject if accuracy>50m
tasks.notes: Accuracy rejection for others' memories is silent — same response as 'not in range'. Numbers tunable via config table.

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — upsert presence_ping (memory_id, user_id, last_seen_at) for co-presence evaluation
tasks.notes: Upsert with ~3min TTL. No coordinates stored — only boolean proximity outcome. Table auto-purged.

tasks.title: Backend: /scan — build teaser response: no coordinates, no media URLs, only memory_id + thumbnail_url + drop_date + owner_display
tasks.notes: Location submitted → validated → discarded. 204 when nothing nearby. Privacy invariant: no coords ever sent to non-owner.

tasks.title: Backend: /unlock — enforce two-check dwell rule: require two passing proximity checks ≥20s apart (scan counts as check #1)
tasks.notes: Compensates for no velocity/teleport detection. Own memories skip dwell (false positives harmless). Failure: 423 Locked {reason: dwell_required}.

tasks.title: Backend: /unlock — seal evaluation: none, fixed_date, duration, age_based, recurring
tasks.notes: All evaluation server-side at unlock time. age_based: compare owner DOB to open_at_age. recurring: compute next_open window.

tasks.title: Backend: /unlock — condition evaluation: time_of_day, season, weather (15min cache), co_presence, long_absence, nth_return
tasks.notes: Weather lazy-evaluated per geohash. Sunset/sunrise via solar formula (no external API). nth_return: count rows in finds table. Fallback to condition_time_fallback when condition not met.

tasks.title: Backend: /unlock — generate signed GET URL for media (short TTL), record Find in finds table
tasks.notes: URL TTL: 60min (long enough to view, short enough to limit sharing). Raw storage keys never leave API. Find record increments nth_return count.

tasks.title: iOS: LocationEngine module — CLLocationManager wrapper, foreground fix acquisition, accuracy reporting, movement gate (>25m or >30s)
tasks.notes: Movement gate prevents scan spam. Reports accuracy_m alongside coordinates.

tasks.title: iOS: Wander mode — foreground scan loop: movement-gated POST /scan calls, parse teaser response, surface pins
tasks.notes: Scan on >25m movement OR >30s elapsed. Pins appear without coordinates — teaser only.

tasks.title: iOS: Warmth cue — non-directional screen-edge ambient gradient + haptic feedback. No arrow, no compass, no direction.
tasks.notes: WarmthCueOverlay + WarmthHaptics band transitions shipped. Device haptic tuning needs Xcode/device.

tasks.title: iOS: Wander unlock flow — tap pin → proximity check → if in range POST /unlock → show media; if not show teaser + walk closer
tasks.notes: Teaser list + unlock + UnlockedMemorySheet + dwell/423 UX. Map walk-closer deferred (list-first v1).

tasks.title: iOS: Memory Lane — grid of own memories, oldest-first, teaser card (photo, drop date, time-since delta), tap to view full
tasks.notes: Grid + pagination + detail (MapKit drop map for owner) + unlock-at-location for media. List `thumbnail_key` only until CSAM pipeline generates thumbs — placeholder icons for now.

tasks.title: iOS: Offline-but-near UX — when in coarse zone but no connectivity, warmth cue persists + show 'You need a signal to open this'
tasks.notes: Coarse zones pre-fetched on every successful scan. Pin location NOT revealed in offline state. DEC-29.

tasks.title: iOS: Found pins cache — cache unlocked own-memory pins for offline Wander rendering (never cache non-owned coords)
tasks.notes: Only own memories. Non-owned coordinates must never persist on device.

tasks.title: Backend: POST /memories/import — accept cluster list (centroid, capture_date), batch create memories, return memory_ids + signed PUT URLs
tasks.notes: Idempotency key: geohash + capture-date bucket (prevents duplicates on retry). source: imported. private-only enforced — elevation to friends/public returns 422 CANNOT_ELEVATE_IMPORT.

tasks.title: iOS: Import — PHAsset fetch filtered to photos with GPS EXIF, no image bytes read during fetch
tasks.notes: Photos permission required. Only metadata fetched at this stage — no pixel data.

tasks.title: iOS: Import — on-device clustering: grid-snap to ~150m cells, merge adjacent, rank by photo count × recency spread, present ~50 clusters
tasks.notes: PhotoClusterEngine shipped (pure Swift). PHAsset fetch + cluster map UI still TODO.

tasks.title: iOS: Import flow — present cluster map, user selects, strip EXIF per asset, bulk upload via signed PUT URLs, progress UI
tasks.notes: Resumable: idempotency key prevents duplicates. Demo milestone: fresh install → dense map in <2min.

tasks.title: iOS: V2 Treasure Chest — compose flow: photo picker, teaser text, seal picker, recipient list, privacy picker (private/recipients/friends)
tasks.notes: Full seal suite. Conditions are opt-in. Each condition must show what the fallback date will be.

tasks.title: iOS: V4 Note in a Bottle — text composer, GPS auto-set from current location, time-only seal picker
tasks.notes: Text-only — no photo. Location from GPS, not picker.

tasks.title: Backend: Seal evaluation — fixed_date: open when now() >= open_at
Backend: Seal evaluation — duration: open when now() >= created_at + locked_hours
Backend: Seal evaluation — age_based: open when recipient's DOB + open_at_age years <= today
tasks.notes: Requires owner to specify recipient DOB or recipient must have DOB on file.

tasks.title: Backend: Seal evaluation — recurring: open during yearly window_start + window_duration_hours, compute and store next_open
Backend: Condition evaluation — time_of_day: open between after_hour and before_hour (server local time of memory drop location)
tasks.notes: Use timezone derived from drop-point coordinates — not user's current timezone.

tasks.title: Backend: Condition evaluation — season: open when current month is within month_start to month_end range
Backend: Condition evaluation — weather: check cached weather condition (rainy/sunny/snow) per geohash, 15min cache TTL
tasks.notes: Lazy-evaluated: only fetched when someone is at the pin. Sunrise/sunset via solar formula, no external API.

tasks.title: Backend: Condition evaluation — co_presence: satisfied when distinct fresh presence_pings >= required_users within window_minutes
tasks.notes: Satisfaction opens ~10min unlock window for all participants. States: idle → gathering → satisfied → unlocked → reset.

tasks.title: Backend: Condition evaluation — long_absence: open when days since last find >= configured threshold
tasks.notes: Query finds table for last found_at. Requires finds table to be populated correctly.

tasks.title: Backend: Condition evaluation — nth_return: open on exactly the nth visit (count rows in finds table for this memory + user)
iOS: Background — significant-change location service: wake on ~500m+ movement, fetch nearby own pins, re-arm geofences, terminate
tasks.notes: Near-zero battery at steady state. Zero location hardware between wakes.

tasks.title: iOS: Background — CLMonitor region setup: ~14 nearest own pins + ~5 coarse geohash zones with eligible others' content
tasks.notes: iOS caps ~20 regions per app. Others' coarse zones: device knows 'cell 9q8yy has something' — never a point coordinate.

tasks.title: iOS: Background — region rotation on every significant-change wake: evict stale regions, arm nearest set
tasks.notes: 1 region slot kept spare. Rotation logic: sort by distance, take top 19.

tasks.title: iOS: Background — CLVisit monitoring: secondary re-arm trigger on arrive/depart at OS-recognized places
tasks.notes: startMonitoringVisits + didVisit → rotateRegions; CLVisitEvent helper tested.

tasks.title: iOS: Background — on region entry: acquire foreground-quality fix, call /scan, validate server-side, fire push only if in range
tasks.notes: BackgroundRegionScanService + MainTabView wiring; push deferred to ios-apns-registration.

tasks.title: iOS: APNs — register for push notifications, send device token to backend, handle token refresh
tasks.notes: AppDelegate token capture, POST /v1/devices/apns, permission sheet bundles notification ask with Always upgrade.

tasks.title: Backend: APNs push delivery — store device token, send summons/proximity notifications via token-based APNs
tasks.notes: Notification content: generic ('Something is waiting for you'). Never includes memory content or coordinates.

tasks.title: Backend: App Attest validation — verify attestation object at auth/drop/unlock, check receipt, validate environment
Backend: App Attest feature flag — app_attest_required flag allows instant bypass during Apple downtime; bypass is audit-logged
tasks.notes: DEC-22. Bypass is an operational action, not silent. Monitor flag state.

tasks.title: iOS: App Attest — generate attestation key, attest key with Apple, send assertion on drop/unlock, check isSimulatedBySoftware
tasks.notes: Reject drop if sourceInformation.isSimulatedBySoftware = true. iOS 17+ minimum covers this API.

tasks.title: Backend: Rate limiting — per-user limits on /scan, /unlock, /memories (POST); IP-based limit on /auth
tasks.notes: IP + user_id + timestamp sufficient. No coordinates needed for abuse detection.

tasks.title: Backend: Accuracy sanity checks — reject if accuracy_m <= 0 or >= 1000m; reject if timestamp outside ±5min clock skew
tasks.notes: DEC-23. Apply to all location-bearing endpoints.

tasks.title: Backend: Audit log instrumentation — structured events on auth, drop, scan, unlock, attest-bypass; never log coordinates
tasks.notes: CI gate: automated grep confirms no coordinate fields in audit_log migration or insert statements.

tasks.title: Backend: GET /user/export — generate signed archive URL containing all own memories (photos, metadata, no others' coords)
tasks.notes: Async job: package, upload to signed S3 path, return URL. Required for App Store privacy compliance.

tasks.title: Backend: DELETE /user — cascade delete all memories, media, finds, pings, sessions; queue media deletion from S3
Backend: CSAM vendor live — swap stub for real hash-match webhook, implement mandatory reporting per 18 U.S.C. §2258A
tasks.notes: On match: flip scan_status to blocked, hide memory from all users, file report. Vendor-agnostic interface (webhook).

tasks.title: Backend: Location CI test suite — GPX fixtures for approach, drive-by, urban canyon, dwell, re-entry; table-driven bubble + seal + condition tests
tasks.notes: Local Postgres in CI. Seed scripts create dense test geography. [Pure-function table-driven tests for bubble/seal/condition + approach/drive-by/urban-canyon scenarios DONE (60 tests green). DB-backed dwell/re-entry GPX integration (Postgres-in-CI seed) deferred — needs CI Postgres, tracked separately.]

tasks.title: Write App Store reviewer rationale for background-location permission — explain CLMonitor + significant-change architecture
tasks.notes: Write early (M3), not at submission. Background location review is a common rejection point.

tasks.title: TestFlight beta — App Store Connect setup, privacy labels, internal test distribution
DB schema: phone_verifications table + recipients column on memories + friends_graph (user_a, user_b, status)
Backend: Phone verification — send OTP via Twilio SMS, verify, store E.164 number on user record
Backend: Recipient ACL — enforce at /scan and /unlock that recipient's verified phone matches memory's recipient list
Backend: Summons — owner triggers SMS to recipient with deep link + generic preview text (never memory content)
tasks.notes: Deep link → sign-in → memory surfaced on map. Twilio for SMS delivery.

tasks.title: iOS: Summons deep link handling — universal link opens app, authenticates, navigates to memory on map
iOS: Recipient UI — phone number input at drop time, contact picker integration, share card (iMessage/WhatsApp deep link)
Backend: Friends graph — send/accept/reject friend request, mutual connection model, friends-tier discovery at /scan
tasks.notes: Mutual only: both users must accept. friend-tier memories discoverable in proximity once mutual.

tasks.title: Backend: Replies — reply is a full Memory record attached to original pin, submitted at same location, discoverable by original owner on next return
tasks.notes: Place-locked: proximity required to submit a reply. Cannot reply from couch.

tasks.title: iOS: Friends UI — friend request flow, friends list, friends-tier privacy picker in drop compose
iOS: Replies UI — reply composer at unlocked pin, owner's Memory Lane shows reply indicator on next return
Backend: Vault — shared family memory map schema, membership, child-access-at-13 gating, milestone seal support
tasks.notes: $99/yr tier. Premium responses: keep (re-earn) or vault (stays unlocked forever once found).

tasks.title: iOS: Vault UI — family map view, member management, milestone seal composer, StoreKit 2 subscription purchase
Backend: Content screening pipeline for public memories — ML classifier + human moderation queue, auto-hide on flag
tasks.notes: Public drops near sensitive locations (homes, schools) get extra scrutiny. Reportable content auto-hides pending review.

tasks.title: Backend: Velocity/teleport detection for public content — position trail + timestamp analysis (revisit DEC-23)
tasks.notes: Deliberately deferred from Phase 1 (private-only). Requires position trail — evaluate privacy cost vs. abuse reduction at Phase 3 kickoff.

tasks.title: Backend: Public memories — 16+ age gate enforcement at /scan for public tier, content-screened before discoverable
iOS: Public memory discovery UI — public pins in Wander, content reporting flow, age gate enforcement
Backend: Institutional layer — curated layer license (per geography, annual), staff-curated memory trails, anonymized engagement data
tasks.notes: Universities, festivals, tourism boards. No ads inside experience ever.

tasks.title: iOS: Institutional layer UI — curated trail overlay, venue-specific Wander mode, memory trail navigator
Android — full parity with iOS Phase 1–2 feature set
tasks.notes: FCM for push. Re-evaluate MapKit → Maps SDK. Background location model differs significantly.
